title: salix × forbesii
intro: Salix × forbesii, also known as Forbes' willow, is a hybrid plant species within the willow genus, resulting from the cross between Salix alba (white willow) and Salix fragilis (crack willow). The bark and leaves of Salix × forbesii have been used in traditional medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating fever and respiratory issues. Salix × forbesii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of salicin, a compound with similar properties to aspirin.
